competitors.name: KnowBe4
competitors.entry_point: Main Phishing navigation section; campaign type selector at wizard start includes Smishing as a first-class option alongside Email, Vishing, and multi-vector
competitors.setup_friction: Phone numbers must be in user profiles before launching; import via CSV, AD sync, or HRIS integration; phone field is optional per user so partial coverage is common
core_flow: Select campaign type (Smishing) at wizard start
Choose target group with member count preview
Select template from Smishing library (categorized by lure type)
Configure delivery spread and schedule window
Pre-launch review: shows delivery estimate and coverage warnings for missing phone numbers
Launch; results in campaign detail with per-user click breakdown
competitors.error_prevention: Coverage warning at pre-launch review step shows members excluded due to missing phone number; warning not a hard block; delivery spread default reduces obvious simultaneous delivery
competitors.trust_model: Coverage gap shown before launch; per-user results in detail view; bot/scanner click filtering documented as automated but mechanism and filtered count not disclosed to admins
competitors.rbac_notes: Admin roles for campaign creation and template library management; view-only roles can see results but not launch campaigns
competitors.repeat_use_efficiency: Templates saved and reusable; group selections persist; scheduling supports recurring campaigns; smart defaults for delivery spread
competitors.ai_personalization: Not observed — variable token substitution only (first name, company name); no AI-generated personalization for SMS content
competitors.risk_score_surface: SmartRisk Score includes smishing click signals; treated with equivalent weight to email phishing clicks — no evidence of channel-differentiated weighting
competitors.remediation_path: Employee who clicks lands on mobile-optimized debrief page explaining the simulation and missed clues; links to short training module; automated module assignment can be configured for clickers
competitors.source_notes: Based on published help center documentation, product changelog, and publicly available demo recordings. UI details may have changed.

competitors.name: Proofpoint SAT
competitors.entry_point: Security Awareness Training section under Simulations; SMS/voice simulations presented as a subsection — navigation hierarchy signals secondary capability
competitors.setup_friction: Phone numbers sourced from Active Directory attributes or CSV; less flexible import tooling; coverage visualization less prominent
core_flow: Navigate to Simulations, select SMS/Voice subsection
Choose template from smaller SMS library (threat-intelligence-informed lures)
competitors.error_prevention: Pre-launch review present but less detailed on data quality issues; exclusion logic less configurable
competitors.trust_model: Less transparent on partial delivery or bot-click filtering
competitors.rbac_notes: Standard admin/viewer roles; phone data access follows standard user management permissions
competitors.repeat_use_efficiency: Templates reusable; scheduling available; fewer smart defaults than KnowBe4
competitors.ai_personalization: Not observed — template-based with threat intelligence-informed lure selection
competitors.risk_score_surface: SMS clicks contribute to reporting metrics; integration into behavioral risk score not clearly documented
competitors.remediation_path: Debrief landing page on click; mobile optimization quality not clearly documented — inferred to be responsive desktop rather than mobile-first
competitors.source_notes: Based on product documentation and G2 reviews. Some observations inferred from documentation structure. Not directly observed in product UI.

competitors.name: Hoxhunt
competitors.entry_point: Email simulation only — no SMS/smishing simulation observed in public documentation
competitors.ai_personalization: Email-focused adaptive simulation; not observed on SMS channel
competitors.risk_score_surface: Behavioral risk model exists for email signals only
competitors.remediation_path: Email debrief model with gamified reporting; no SMS remediation path observed
competitors.source_notes: Absence of SMS capability based on public documentation and help center review. Treated as email-only.

competitors.name: Cofense
competitors.entry_point: Email phishing simulation specialist; no SMS simulation in product scope based on published documentation
core_flow: Email phishing simulation only; focused on reporter button behavior conditioning
competitors.risk_score_surface: No behavioral risk score; focused on reporting rate metrics
competitors.remediation_path: Reporter button integration; email debrief only
competitors.source_notes: Cofense product scope confirms email-only simulation. Included to document depth-over-breadth positioning.

observed_patterns: First-class channel selector at wizard start positions SMS as peer to email (KnowBe4)
Phone number coverage warning before launch — warning not a hard block (KnowBe4)
Template library organized by lure type (IT, HR, financial, delivery) not industry (KnowBe4)
Delivery spread / randomized send window as configurable option (KnowBe4)
Mobile-optimized debrief landing page for smishing click (KnowBe4)
Threat-intelligence-informed template lures correlated with active real-world campaigns (Proofpoint)
Per-user result breakdown in campaign detail view (KnowBe4)
anti_patterns: SMS as a secondary navigation item buried under a submenu — signals secondary capability, reduces admin adoption (Proofpoint)
Template-only personalization marketed as AI-driven — fails credibility test for enterprise security teams (KnowBe4, Proofpoint)
Silent partial delivery — missing phone numbers excluded without prominent count in campaign results (observed across all)
Desktop debrief served responsively to mobile — poor experience at the critical learning moment (inferred from Proofpoint)
Flat risk signal treatment — SMS click equated to email click with no channel-specific weighting (inferred from KnowBe4)
differentiation_opportunities: Genuine AI personalization for SMS: adapt spear phishing engine to 160-character constraint — no competitor observed doing this
Channel-aware risk scoring: model email and SMS susceptibility as separate behavioral signals
Coordinated multi-channel simulation: priming email followed by follow-up SMS in a single campaign scenario
Phone number coverage health as a persistent, first-class metric in group management UI
Transparent bot-click filtering with admin visibility: show filtered count and explanation — builds trust in result integrity
design_implications: Campaign wizard must present SMS as a first-class channel type at the top-level step
Phone number coverage must surface at group selection step and in campaign results
Template library needs categories grounded in realistic lure types with threat intelligence freshness signals
Message editor must show live character counter with encoding detection
Delivery spread should be a smart default, not an advanced option
Mobile debrief landing page is a new design surface — 375px viewport, Pause/Verify/Report framework
Risk score treatment should be visible in reporting UI rather than silently blended
If v1 is template-based only, use Smart Templates framing — not AI framing
confidence_notes: KnowBe4 observations are medium-high confidence based on published documentation and demo recordings
Proofpoint SAT observations are medium confidence; some inferences from documentation structure and G2 reviews
Hoxhunt SMS absence is medium confidence — no SMS simulation found in public materials
Cofense email-only is medium-high confidence based on explicit product positioning
No direct product session access was used for any competitor
